name: Via Verde del Segura — round trip through the Zaframagon Rock Nature Reserve
name_original: Vía Verde del Segura — ida y vuelta por la Reserva Natural del Peñón de Zaframagón
region_intro: Hiking around Olvera, a "white village" in Andalusia, Spain, offers diverse outdoor experiences. The region is characterized by rolling hills, dramatic geological formations, and the unique Vía Verde de la Sierra, an abandoned railway line converted into a path. This landscape includes natural reserves and is situated near larger natural parks, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Olvera The most popular hiking route is Via Verde del Segura — round trip through the Zaframagon Rock Nature Reserve, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route follows an old railway line, featuring tunnels and viaducts, and passes through the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve, known for its Griffon vulture colony. Another top favourite among local hikers is Andalusia Square in Olvera loop from Olvera, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the town and surrounding countryside, including the historic Moorish Castle and the Iglesia Parroquial de Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación. Local hikers also love the Andalusia Square in Olvera – La Victoria Church loop from Mirador San Diego, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through the town's historic areas and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Olvera is defined by rolling hills, abandoned railway lines, and dramatic geological formations, with options ranging from easy walks to more challenging trails. The network offers routes suitable for various ability levels, including 8 easy, 7 moderate, and 1 difficult trail. The routes in Olvera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 500 reviews. More than 2,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Olvera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Olvera?
A: Olvera offers a diverse network of 16 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 8 easy, 7 moderate, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Olvera?
A: Yes, Olvera has 8 easy trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14590385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andalusia Square in Olvera – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.4 km long and takes about 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle introduction to the area's scenic beauty.

Q: What are the best circular hiking routes in Olvera?
A: Many routes around Olvera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009552364/via-verde-del-segura-round-trip-through-the-zaframagon-rock-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Segura — round trip through the Zaframagon Rock Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 12.6 km loop that takes you through stunning landscapes and the famous vulture colony. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31482224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olvera – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;, a 11.4 km moderate trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Olvera?
A: Olvera's hikes offer a wealth of natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the unique Vía Verde de la Sierra with its tunnels and viaducts, pass through the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve to spot Griffon vultures, or enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding countryside from the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Olvera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archpriest Church of Our Lady of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt;. The Rock of the Coroneta also offers accessible scenic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Olvera?
A: Yes, Olvera has several family-friendly routes, especially among its 8 easy trails. The Vía Verde de la Sierra, being a relatively flat path along an old railway line, is particularly suitable for families with children. These trails offer a chance to enjoy nature and spot wildlife in a safe and accessible environment.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Olvera?
A: Many trails in the Olvera region are dog-friendly, especially those on the Vía Verde de la Sierra and surrounding rural paths. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in nature reserves like Peñón de Zaframagón, to protect local wildlife, especially the Griffon vultures. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olvera?
A: The best time to hike in Olvera is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, with less intense heat than the summer months and generally less rain than winter. Spring also brings wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful colors.

Q: Are there any opportunities to see wildlife, especially birds, while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Olvera is a prime location for wildlife observation. The Vía Verde de la Sierra passes directly through the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve, which hosts the largest colony of Griffon vultures in Europe. Hikers often have excellent opportunities to see these magnificent birds soaring overhead. The proximity to Grazalema and Sierra de las Nieves Natural Parks also means a chance to spot ibex mountain goats and wild boar.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Olvera?
A: The hiking trails around Olvera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views, the unique experience of the Vía Verde de la Sierra with its tunnels and viaducts, and the opportunity to observe Griffon vultures. The diverse terrain and well-maintained paths also receive positive feedback.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites to visit near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Olvera is rich in history. The town itself is dominated by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Olvera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archpriest Church of Our Lady of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt;, both offering incredible views. Further afield, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2973761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zahara de la Sierra Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6247642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Cañete la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which are also significant historical landmarks in the wider region.

Q: Are there any lakes or viewpoints near Olvera's hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region around Olvera features beautiful lakes and numerous viewpoints. While hiking, you can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5605632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5605641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View over the lake and white villages&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4110752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable body of water, and viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5605657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint overlooking the lake and Zahara’s white houses&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas of the landscape.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Olvera?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Olvera, especially near the starting points of popular trails like the Vía Verde de la Sierra. For routes starting directly from the town, you can often find street parking or designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions or fees.

The Guadalporcún River rises in the Sierra Norte of the province of Cádiz in the municipality of Alcalá del Valle, and flows into the Guadalete River in the vicinity of Puerto Serrano. The river is not regulated, so the flow regime is natural.
